Magnetic Dirk of Distraction  
 

Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY
Skill: 1H Piercing Atk Delay: 23
DMG: 8 Dmg Bonus: 35
Backstab DMG: 8
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STR: +4 DEX: +10
SV MAGIC: +5
Effect: Distraction (Combat, Casting Time: Instant)
WT: 3.0 Size: SMALL
Class: WAR RNG BRD ROG BST
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)
Magnetic Dirk of Distraction
Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY
Skill: 1H Piercing Atk Delay: 23
DMG: 8 Dmg Bonus: 35
Backstab DMG: 8
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STR: +4 DEX: +10
SV MAGIC: +5
Effect: Distraction (Combat, Casting Time: Instant)
WT: 3.0 Size: SMALL
Class: WAR RNG BRD ROG BST
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Item Lore:The Dirk faces north
Item Type:1H Piercing
Stackable:No
Lucy Entry By:Kerasota
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-12-11 09:05:05
Page Updated:Thu Oct 9th, 2008

Expansion: Scars of Velious
Rarity: Uncommon
Level to Attain: 43
[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Tower of Frozen Shadow
NPC Name
Cara Omica


Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Tower of Frozen Shadow


Uploaded November 27th, 2008

I don't use this weapon myself, but my tip would be to keep facing north while you attack a mob with it, to reduce the annoyance.
▲ Page top
Here's a thought people...
QuoteReply
# Apr 14 2002 at 12:45 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591777168162
View User Forum
1 post
If the proc is REALLY crippling you, target the main tank during battle and hit autofollow when you're behind a single mob... you'll always face the mob that way, proc or no proc.
▲ Page top
Has it's uses
QuoteReply
# Oct 12 2001 at 11:14 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592757364712
33 posts
I looted this little weapon months ago (before I knew what it did) and then came here to find out about it. Being that I already had a Trak Tooth, this simply sat in my bag as a "I was there and looted this" type status symbol. Well our guild recently did another ToFS raid and we ended up fighting Tserrina Syl'Tor on the 7th floor. Suddenly I had every piece of sv magic item I could find on me. We positioned everyoe on the North side of her and let me stick to the South so I could backstab without fear or suddenly spinning away. There was a special benefit to fighting her in the far back cubby hole there. I was only blinded once, but there was a trap in the back (which also faced North) so I simply had to tap my sense traps hotkey to ensure I was still facing the correct way. We killed her around 4 times before I logged that night. I resisted (or was out of range of) every spell except the one blind spell. I also ended up with the Whip that procs Muscle Lock I. So now I have another weapon that sits in my bag. (Personally I find the Serrated Bone Dirk to be a better slowing weapon considering you wont need to swap it to backstab. And I can make a stronger version of Muscle Lock anyway.) So right now this Dirk falls into the resist gear section. But that is still better than being totally useless.
▲ Page top
Good lil weap
QuoteReply
# Aug 22 2001 at 11:28 AM Rating: Decent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Thing about this weapon is that its not only for a rogue. There are so many rogues getting pissed off about this weapon, but as a ranger, it is a fine 2nd for the GBS. You get 10 dex, 4str and more - plus a decent ratio. My lvl 35 ranger would love to use something like this. That dex is longed for especially when kiting with my crazy crazy bow. Anything that makes me crit more often with my bow is good in my book, and adding 10 to dex is great. No, it definitely is not the best weapon, and yes - I Did compare to the GBS which is a much better weapon (only comparison was because of the dex - but hey, if you have the money to spend on a GBS then fine - go for it - and if you are at the lvl to go for the GBS you shouldnt be going for this anyway, right?). But the ratio is not very bad, and I am sure a ranger of my lvl who appreciates speed and dex buffs would be completely happy wielding something like this. Hell, we use DH yaks that proc every once and awhile, and their ratio is just a smidge worse than this thing. I think its a great little weapon - maybe not the top piercer for a rogue, but then again its not rogue only.

Just my 2 cents.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jul 18 2001 at 5:10 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Could anyone please post the quest to have the curse removed?
Observations
QuoteReply
# Jul 04 2001 at 1:14 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591761757400
9 posts
Just my observations on using this weapon as a 51 warrior (sorry, I'm not uber).

It does make you point true North. Ok, but an intelligent poster said that if you stand South of the mob you should be fine. While this isn't ideal for rogues in particular(hey, the mob could be facing South!), I'm quite enjoying this dagger as it DOES cause quite a bit of pain for the intended mob.

I have been stabbing with this dagger for an average of 28 a pop (off a 8/23!), and I have crit for 50+s. While this may not be a big deal for most of you, I would relate that this weapon causes more damage than my Cane of Tranquil does (11/22). Even my Kunzar (8/22) cannot outdamage this hidden beauty. And yes I have accounted for the Dex/Str differences.

I'm not sure about questing the dagger to reverse the curse, but for the current moment I'm quite happy using it as a main. I mean, if you have info for the dagger's quest, then by all means post it, so then another fine weapon doesn't get its reputation trashed.

* Just an update - Cane of the Tranquil is 11/25, sorry.

Also, I've been noticing lately that the dagger is beginning to make the mob turn around to face true North. It hasn't done this frequently, but every now and then the mob turns to face North for about half a second. I'm sure a rogue would appreciate that. *
▲ Page top

When you loot the dagger it is curesed you have to do a quest to make it uncuresed once uncuresed it will spin the target to face north

#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 16 2001 at 3:13 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) OK guys i have only a lv35 warrior and i got one of these peices of crap the other night... and was very made when i found out what the proc does.... but with a little hard word you guys will find the guy that will remove this curse and have it spin the mobs so there back is to.. or should i say have them face nother....no for all you giving this weapon hell... start looking.. i found it and so can you...Just wish i could BS.
Thanks Verant
QuoteReply
# May 12 2001 at 5:39 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591755483706
1 post
I have one of these I got on a guild raid at the tower. I just wanted to post this to thank Verant again for another rogue frustration. I was very glad to have this to duel weild with my SBD untill the proc worked. Why ruin a good weapon when it is so hard to get a decent rogue piercer as it is? BTW to you above, the proc spins YOU around not the MOB, also it is not random, you always face north hence magnetic,,, imagine that huh? well anyways that is my post. If anyone can give me advise on a good weapon that isn't cursed/delay199/no longer dropped I'd be very thankfull,

Tatsuoo Treasurehunter
"Im NOT a THIEF! I'm a TREASUREHUNTER"
"I didn't pickpoceket!... these are just poor Hill Giants. "

44th Rogue of Bristlebane
▲ Page top
Proc
QuoteReply
# May 07 2001 at 8:53 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Ok, all the info above is wrong, the weapon interrupts just bout 90% of the casting of the target. It is very usefull for any of the classes that are allowed to use it.

Vlorn
52 Enchanter
Torvo
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: May 24 2001 at 10:54 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Umm, for a 52nd level, you sure don't know much. As someone who has one of these, I know exactly how it works, and it causes the WIELDER to face true north. If it's disrupting anything, it's because of the speed of the weapon. Please refrain from speaking about something you know nothing about.
It gets worse
QuoteReply
# Apr 24 2001 at 12:56 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591754221262
13 posts
I just got one of these daggers last night off Cara Omica. Not only is it cursed, but it's primary only. At the level you have to be to get this, you should already have a higher damage weapon making this one totally worthless. Speaking for myself, I can't picture giving up backstabbing for over 250 just to get a few strength and dexterity points. If this really was allowed in the secondary slot, it wouldn't be so bad. One could learn to tolerate the stupid proc. :)

▲ Page top
Dirk is magnetic for a reason...
QuoteReply
# Feb 02 2001 at 11:18 AM Rating: Good
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
silly people, the dirk is nagnetic for a reason, the proc on it, Distraction is only distracting if you don't understand what it is.
have somebody identify it for you, you'll get this:
Item Lore: The Dirk points north.
Distraction IS true north spell. just stand south side of mobs and yer fine, proc no longer becomes a problem.

Elarian Tinesthai
▲ Page top
RE: Dirk is magnetic for a reason...
QuoteReply
# Mar 30 2001 at 4:45 PM Rating: Default

Anonymous
AHH, Thank you, this thing has been sittin in the bank since I got it. I will not get it back out. I am glad someone was paying attention.
▲ Page top
Warning
QuoteReply
# Feb 01 2001 at 10:55 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
This item is completely worthless.The effect is against the weilder plus it is no drop.With the added dex it procs even more during a fight so unless you just enjoy constantly having to search you target down while fighting pass this bad boy up. In my opinion it is a waste of toime to put a weapon like this in the game its not range, its no drop and a complete pain in the rump when using it.
▲ Page top
RE: Warning
QuoteReply
# Mar 21 2001 at 2:27 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I think you've missed the whole point of this weapon. I imagine they had Rogues in mind with this but thought to add to a couple other classes anyways to equip (but rogues are the ones that can use it well). If your a rogue & lets say group with a Rng, Dru, Nec, SK - this item is great. Reason is the other members snare. The critter starts to run & leaves their back totally exposed to the rogue to have a free for all stab'n good time. Rogues even get some darkness items too. With this weapon they could conceivably solo blues. So the proc isn't a complete waste of time. Its how you use it that counts :-)
▲ Page top
RE: Warning
QuoteReply
# Feb 14 2001 at 4:53 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
like the glowing black sword. and sooo many other items, they make something decent and then give it some idiotic effect that ruins it.

RE: Warning
QuoteReply
# Mar 12 2004 at 6:33 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592343673941
161 posts
Glowing black sword on PVP for a pure unbuffed melee is nice to have. Especially as a non casting Warrior or Rogue. It removes bad effects also. just so you know. This weapon maybe is ment to be thus. Decent, decent delay, with a pain in the but curse. The more people post and complain, the more someone in Verant gets a chuckle I am sure.

111 posts
whirl till you hurl is a pb stun... the mob just halts, and there never is any "spinning so they cant attack"... maybe for roleplaying sake thats what the spell is supposed to do, but when my enchanter casts it, thats not what actually happens.

Cursed Item
QuoteReply
# Jan 12 2001 at 4:41 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I don't have one of these nor have i seen one, however I just wanted to mention that prior to the release of SoV verant made a post to the forums stating that we should expect to see some realy nice items with curses on them. Form what I read above this seems to fit the bill perfectly. An annoying proc but not a killer really. Nicely thought out item in my opinion as for the level a 10 dex add is VERY nice.
